AbortMultipartUpload.name: AbortMultipartUpload
AbortMultipartUpload.documentation: Cancels an in-progress multipart upload for the specified object in the specified bucket. After cancellation, no additional parts can be uploaded using that upload ID and storage consumed by previously uploaded parts is released.

CompleteMultipartUpload.name: CompleteMultipartUpload
CompleteMultipartUpload.documentation: Finalizes a multipart upload by assembling all previously uploaded parts into a single object. The request body must include the list of parts with their part numbers and ETag values in ascending order.

CopyObject.name: CopyObject
CopyObject.documentation: Creates a server-side copy of an existing object within the same bucket or across buckets. The source object is identified through the x-amz-copy-source header. Nutanix Objects supports the STANDARD storage class only.

CreateBucket.name: CreateBucket
CreateBucket.documentation: Creates a new bucket with the specified name. The authenticated user becomes the owner of the bucket.

CreateBucketMetadataTableConfiguration.name: CreateBucketMetadataTableConfiguration
CreateBucketMetadataTableConfiguration.documentation: Nutanix Objects does not support this operation.

CreateMultipartUpload.name: CreateMultipartUpload

CreateSession.name: CreateSession
CreateSession.documentation: Nutanix Objects does not support this operation.

DeleteBucket.name: DeleteBucket

DeleteBucketAnalyticsConfiguration.name: DeleteBucketAnalyticsConfiguration

DeleteBucketCors.name: DeleteBucketCors

DeleteObjectTagging.name: DeleteObjectTagging

DeleteObjects.name: DeleteObjects

UploadPart.name: UploadPart
UploadPart.documentation: Uploads a single part in a multipart upload. Each request must specify the upload ID and a sequential part number between 1 and 10,000.

UploadPartCopy.name: UploadPartCopy
UploadPartCopy.documentation: Copies data from an existing object as a part in a multipart upload. The source object and byte range are specified through request headers.

WriteGetObjectResponse.name: WriteGetObjectResponse

PutBucketNfsConfiguration.name: PutBucketNfsConfiguartion
PutBucketNfsConfiguration.documentation: Sets the NFS access configuration for the specified bucket. NFS-configured buckets support both S3 and NFS protocol access. This is a Nutanix Objects-specific operation.

GetBucketNfsConfiguration.name: GetBucketNfsConfiguration
GetBucketNfsConfiguration.documentation: Retrieves the NFS access configuration of the specified bucket. This is a Nutanix Objects-specific operation.

PutBucketInventoryStatus.name: PutBucketInventoryStatus

GetBucketInventoryStatus.name: GetBucketInventoryStatus

ListFederationMembers.name: ListFederationMembers
ListFederationMembers.documentation: Returns the list of federation members in the configured namespace. This is a Nutanix Objects-specific operation for federated object store deployments.

ExpectedBucketOwner.documentation: The expected owner of the bucket. The request fails if the actual owner does not match.

MFADelete.documentation: Specifies whether MFA delete is active for the versioning configuration.

ErrorDocument.documentation: The error document configuration for website hosting.

IndexDocument.documentation: The index document configuration for website hosting.

RedirectAllRequestsTo.documentation: Redirects all requests to the specified target.

RoutingRules.documentation: The list of website redirect routing rules.

ErrorCode.documentation: The error code returned by the service.

AcceptRanges.documentation: Indicates that a range of bytes was served.

CacheControl.documentation: The Cache-Control header value for the object.

ContentDisposition.documentation: The Content-Disposition header value for the object.

ContentEncoding.documentation: The Content-Encoding header value for the object.

ContentLanguage.documentation: The Content-Language header value for the object.

ContentLength.documentation: The size of the object in bytes.

ContentRange.documentation: The byte range of the object included in the response.

ContentType.documentation: The Content-Type header value (MIME type) for the object.

ChecksumCRC32.documentation: The base64-encoded CRC-32 checksum of the object.

ChecksumCRC32C.documentation: The base64-encoded CRC-32C checksum of the object.

ChecksumSHA1.documentation: The base64-encoded SHA-1 checksum of the object.

ChecksumSHA256.documentation: The base64-encoded SHA-256 checksum of the object.

DeleteMarker.documentation: Indicates whether the object is a delete marker.

ETag.documentation: The entity tag (ETag) of the object, which is a hash of the object content.

Expires.documentation: The date and time after which the object is no longer cacheable.

Expiration.documentation: The expiration information for the object. Contains lifecycle rule ID and expiration date.

LastModified.documentation: The date and time the object was last modified.

Metadata.documentation: The user-defined metadata for the object.

ObjectLockLegalHoldStatus.documentation: The Object Lock legal hold status (ON or OFF).

ObjectLockRetainUntilDate.documentation: The date and time until which the object is retained.

ServerSideEncryption.documentation: The server-side encryption algorithm used for the object.

SSECustomerAlgorithm.documentation: The server-side encryption algorithm for customer-provided keys.

SSEKMSKeyId.documentation: The identifier of the KMS key for server-side encryption.

SSECustomerKeyMD5.documentation: The MD5 digest of the customer-provided encryption key.

StorageClass.documentation: The storage class for the object. Nutanix Objects supports the STANDARD storage class only.

TagCount.documentation: The number of tags associated with the object.

VersionId.documentation: The version identifier of the object.

BucketKeyEnabled.documentation: Indicates whether an S3 Bucket Key is used for server-side encryption.

CoolOffThresholdSecs.documentation: The cool-off threshold in seconds before data becomes eligible for tiering.

UID.documentation: The POSIX user ID for NFS access. This is a Nutanix Objects-specific parameter.

GID.documentation: The POSIX group ID for NFS access. This is a Nutanix Objects-specific parameter.

GID.documentation: The POSIX group ID for NFS access. This is a Nutanix Objects-specific parameter.

Directory.documentation: Indicates whether the object represents a directory.

Errors.documentation: The list of errors from the operation.

InventoryConfigs.documentation: The list of inventory configurations to set.

Quiet.documentation: Indicates whether the response includes only errors.

InventoryConfigIdentifier.documentation: A unique identifier for an inventory configuration.

UpdatedInventoryConfigIdentifier.documentation: Contains updated inventory config identifier information.

Errors.documentation: The list of errors from the operation.

InventoryConfigs.documentation: The list of inventory configurations to set.

Lookup.documentation: Contains inventory configuration identifiers to look up.

VersionId.documentation: The version identifier of the object.

ReadInventoryConfigIdentifier.documentation: A unique identifier for reading inventory configuration.

FederationName.documentation: The name of the federation. This is a Nutanix Objects-specific parameter.

FederationMembers.documentation: The list of federation members. This is a Nutanix Objects-specific parameter.

FederationReleaseVersion.documentation: The release version of the federation member. This is a Nutanix Objects-specific parameter.

Accelerate.documentation: Enables this client to use S3 Transfer Acceleration endpoints.

DisableMultiRegionAccessPoints.documentation: Disables this client's usage of Multi-Region Access Points.

DisableS3ExpressSessionAuth.documentation:
Disables this client's usage of Session Auth for S3Express
      buckets and reverts to using conventional SigV4 for those.

ForcePathStyle.documentation: Forces this client to use path-style addressing for buckets.

UseArnRegion.documentation: Enables this client to use an ARN's region when constructing an endpoint instead of the client's configured region.
